methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $14.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Quantum Computing. Range: $4.5B - $13.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

data.narrative:
Quantinuum, a Series G quantum computing company founded by Ilyas Khan, has raised over $2.7B and carries a current post-money valuation of $14.8B. The valuation peer set comprises three publicly traded quantum computing firms—IonQ ($13.0B), D-Wave Quantum ($6.3B), and Rigetti Computing ($4.5B)—alongside four additional private-stage comparables.

Two triangulation methods anchor the analysis. Stage-progression modeling applies a typical 1.25x step-up multiplier to the $14.8B last post-money valuation, yielding $18.5B. Peer-median analysis across the three public quantum firms produces a $6.3B implied value (median), with a range of $4.5B to $13.0B. The divergence between methods reflects material heterogeneity in the quantum computing sector and valuation uncertainty around private versus public quantum company trajectories.

Synthesizing both approaches yields a triangulated range of $5.3B (low) to $21.2B (high), with a median of $12.4B. The current $14.8B valuation sits comfortably within this band, slightly above the two-method median. Key uncertainties include the persistence of the public-private quantum valuation differential, near-term commercialization prospects across the peer set, and whether Quantinuum's technological positioning justifies a premium to the peer median of $6.3B. Further due diligence on revenue run-rate and customer concentration would narrow the range.
